items.title: What are the main events for today?
items.summary: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we don't have much on the agenda other than a couple of low tier releases like the Italian final CPI and the German ZEW index. The data won't change anything for the ECB, so the market reaction will likely be muted. Moreover, we can expect sentiment indices like the ZEW or PMIs to improve in the next months on easing rate hike expectations and lower energy prices. AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, the only highlight is the US housing starts and building permits report. This is not market-moving data and won't change anything for the Fed. The markets are now focused on the positive effects stemming from the US-Iran deal and the official end of the war. Expectations of lower oil prices are easing inflation concerns and leading to a dovish repricing in interest rates. The short-term effect is certainly positive for growth and risk assets like stocks are the main beneficiaries. Looking ahead, the question will be whether this negative supply shock turns into a positive demand one where economic activity strengthens significantly and keeps inflationary pressure high eventually forcing the Fed to intervene with rate hikes. This is likely going to be the next tail risk for markets. CENTRAL BANK SPEAKERS 10:55 GMT/06:55 ET - ECB's Escriva (neutral - voter) 13:10 GMT/09:10 ET - ECB's Lane (neutral - voter) 19:05 GMT/15:05 ET - ECB's Sleijpen (neutral - voter) This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 欧洲时段 在欧洲时段，除了意大利最终CPI和德国ZEW指数等几项低关注度数据外，日程上没有太多值得关注的内容。这些数据不会对欧洲央行的政策产生任何影响，因此市场反应预计将较为温和。此外，随着加息预期放缓和能源价格走低，预计未来几个月ZEW或PMI等市场信心指数将有所改善。美股时段 在美股时段，唯一的亮点是美国新屋开工和建筑许可报告。这些数据不会引发市场波动，也不会改变美联储的政策走向。目前市场关注的焦点是美伊协议带来的积极影响以及战争的正式结束。油价走低的预期正在缓解通胀担忧，并促使市场对利率进行鸽派重估。短期来看，这对经济增长无疑是利好的，而股票等风险资产将成为主要受益者。展望未来，关键问题在于：这场负面的供给冲击是否会转变为正面的需求冲击——即经济活动显著增强，通胀压力持续高企，最终迫使美联储通过加息进行干预。 这很可能将成为市场面临的下一个尾部风险。央行官员讲话 格林尼治标准时间10:55/美国东部时间06:55 - 欧洲央行埃斯克里瓦（中性立场——有投票权） 格林尼治标准时间 13:10/美国东部时间 09:10 - 欧洲央行莱恩（中性立场——有投票权） 格林尼治标准时间 19:05/美国东部时间 15:05 - 欧洲央行斯莱彭（中性立场——有投票权） 本文作者为 investinglive.com 的朱塞佩·德拉莫塔。

AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, the only highlight is the US housing starts and building permits report. This is not market-moving data and won't change anything for the Fed. The markets are now focused on the positive effects stemming from the US-Iran deal and the official end of the war. Looking ahead, the question will be whether this negative supply shock turns into a positive demand one where economic activity strengthens significantly and keeps inflationary pressure high eventually forcing the Fed to intervene with rate hikes. This is likely going to be the next tail risk for markets. CENTRAL BANK SPEAKERS 10:55 GMT/06:55 ET - ECB's Escriva (neutral - voter) 13:10 GMT/09:10 ET - ECB's Lane (neutral - voter) 19:05 GMT/15:05 ET - ECB's Sleijpen (neutral - voter) This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.

items.title: What are the main events for today?
items.summary: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, the main highlight was the UK CPI report. The data came out lower than expected vindicating the BoE's patient stance and will likely see traders paring back further BoE rate hike bets considering also the drop in oil prices. Looking ahead, we don't have much on the agenda other than the final Eurozone CPI report. The data won't change anything for the ECB at this point, so the market reaction will likely be muted. AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, we have the US Retail Sales report and the FOMC rate decision. The US Retail Sales M/M is expected at 0.5% vs 0.5% prior, while the Ex-Autos M/M metric is seen at 0.5% vs 0.7% prior. The Control Group M/M is expected at 0.4% vs 0.5% prior. This is generally a market-moving release but it's almost always faded because of the volatile nature of the data. The main event today is the FOMC rate decision. The Fed is widely expected to keep the federal funds rate unchanged at 3.50-3.75% and remove the easing bias from the statement. No dissenter is expected. At this meeting, we also get the Summary of Economic Projections (SEP) where near-term inflation is expected to be revised higher and unemployment lower. The dot plot is expected to show no cuts for this year. The focus will be on the dot plot and Fed Chair Warsh's press conference. CENTRAL BANK SPEAKERS 13:00 GMT/09:00 ET - ECB's Sleijpen (neutral - voter) This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 欧洲时段 欧洲时段的主要焦点是英国CPI报告。该数据低于预期，印证了英国央行（BoE）的耐心立场，加之油价下跌，交易员可能会进一步削减对英国央行加息的押注。展望未来，除了欧元区最终CPI报告外，日程上没有太多重要事件。目前该数据不会对欧洲央行政策产生任何影响，因此市场反应预计将较为温和。 美国时段 美国时段将公布美国零售销售报告及美联储联邦公开市场委员会（FOMC）利率决议。预计美国零售销售环比增长0.5%（前值0.5%），而剔除汽车后的零售销售环比增长预计为0.5%（前值0.7%）。控制组环比数据预计为0.4%，前值为0.5%。该数据通常会引发市场波动，但由于数据波动性较大，其影响往往很快消退。今日的重头戏是FOMC利率决议。市场普遍预期美联储将维持联邦基金利率在3.50%-3.75%不变，并从声明中移除宽松倾向。预计不会有反对票。 在本次会议上，我们还将获得《经济预测摘要》（SEP），预计其中短期通胀预期将上调，失业率预期将下调。点阵图预计将显示今年不会降息。市场焦点将集中在点阵图以及美联储主席沃什的新闻发布会上。央行发言人 格林尼治标准时间13:00/美国东部时间09:00——欧洲央行斯莱彭（中性立场——有投票权） 本文作者为investinglive.com的朱塞佩·德拉莫塔。

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: US weighs navy, insurance moves to transit Hormuz
items.summary: PBOC governor Pan Gongsheng signals slower credit growth and offshore FX push China to issue CNY 300bln bonds to boost bank capital as local debt tackled Goldman holds TTF gas forecasts, flags upside risk and delayed LNG recovery China financial regulator vows to tackle property, local debt and small bank risks US Defense Production Act floated to force US insurer to cover Hormuz passage, Navy escort PBOC sets USD/ CNY mid-point today at 6.8096 (vs. estimate at 6.7569) Japan exports beat forecasts in May at fastest pace since late 2022 Singapore May exports surge 38.4%, biggest jump in 20 years on AI demand Japan April core machinery orders surge, exports beat also "OpenAI burned $3.7 billion in first quarter of 2026" One more ECB hike seen in September before rates plateau through 2027 Japan business sentiment rises in June on semiconductor demand, Reuters Tankan Chinese export flood puts EU tariff wall firmly on G7 agenda in France New Zealand Q1 current account deficit narrows sharply on quarterly basis US-Iran deal: where things stand and what comes next Yen stays weak after BoJ hike as analysts eye intervention risk New Zealand consumer confidence drops to lowest since 2023, survey shows investingLive Americas market news wrap: Oil continues to fall in the lead-up to the FOMC Oil: Private inventory survey shows a headline crude oil draw much greater than expected RBA holds rates at 4.35%, signals further hikes still possible - recap Broader US indices close near lows. Dow industrial average closes higher. Summary: US weighing naval escorts and insurance mandates to unblock nearly 500 stranded tankers at Hormuz despite MOU signing; oil prices lower on supply return expectations New Zealand consumer confidence hit a two-year low of 80.4 in Q2; Q1 current account deficit narrowed sharply to NZ$1.008B from NZ$5.984B prior Japan Reuters Tankan: manufacturers +13 from +8, non-manufacturers +32 from +29; Nikkei clawed back losses to print a fresh all-time high near 70,000 Singapore NODX surged 38.4% in May, a 20-year high, on AI electronics demand; US 12.5% tariff proposal clouds the outlook China vice premier announced CNY 300bln special bond issue to recapitalise banks and pledged vigorous local government debt resolution; PBOC governor Pan Gongsheng signalled credit growth will not return to its previous pace and laid groundwork for further yuan internationalisation OpenAI burned through $3.7bln in Q1 2026, more than half its $5.7bln in revenue, per Reuters citing The Information; confidential IPO prospectus filed last week with a suspected September debut FX markets subdued ahead of the FOMC; Chinese equities lagged with the Hang Seng -0.7% and Shanghai Comp -0.2% as auto and aluminium names weighed and markets shrugged off reports the US delayed blacklisting DeepSeek and over 100 Chinese firms Markets drifted towards the North American session in a holding pattern, with the Federal Open Market Committee decision the dominant event risk and traders reluctant to extend positions ahead of new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first major policy test at the podium. The Hormuz supply picture remained the key macro overhang for energy markets. Oil prices traded lower as the US-Iran interim peace deal continued to price in a gradual return of supply, though the physical reality remains complicated: nearly 500 vessels including 220 oil tankers are sitting anchored outside the strait, unable to move while insurance markets remain effectively closed to Hormuz transits. The Trump administration is now weighing fee-based naval escorts and potential use of the Defense Production Act to compel US insurers to provide coverage, with the White House under pressure to translate the MOU into actual barrel flows. Out of the Asia-Pacific session, the data run was broadly constructive. Japan's Reuters Tankan showed two consecutive months of improving manufacturer sentiment, Singapore delivered its strongest export print in two decades on AI-driven chip demand, and New Zealand's current account deficit narrowed sharply on a quarterly basis. The softer note came from New Zealand consumer confidence, which fell to its lowest reading since 2023 as war-related fuel costs and borrowing pressures bit into household sentiment. China generated significant headline flow. The vice premier's CNY 300 billion special bond announcement to recapitalise financial institutions, paired with a pledge to vigorously resolve local government debt, represented the most concrete fiscal commitment in some time. PBOC governor Pan Gongsheng followed with a frank assessment that sustaining China's previous pace of credit growth is neither achievable nor desirable, while also advancing the groundwork for broader yuan internationalisation through new offshore FX and bond market steps in Shanghai. In equities, Japan's Nikkei 225 was the standout, taking back early losses to print a fresh all-time high. Chinese markets lagged, with the Hang Seng off 0.7% and the Shanghai Composite down 0.2%. Report that the US had delayed blacklisting DeepSeek and over 100 Chinese firms failed to lift sentiment. Away from macro, OpenAI's finances came into focus after Reuters, citing The Information, reported the company burned through $3.7 billion in the first quarter of 2026, more than half its $5.7 billion in revenue. The disclosure follows last week's filing of a confidential IPO prospectus, with a market debut suspected for September. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 中国人民银行行长潘功胜暗示信贷增速放缓，离岸外汇市场推动中国发行3000亿人民币债券以增强银行资本金，同时着手解决地方政府债务问题；高盛维持TTF天然气价格预测，指出上行风险并预计液化天然气复苏将推迟；中国金融监管机构誓言解决房地产、地方政府债务及小型银行风险；美国拟动用《国防生产法》迫使美国保险公司承保霍尔木兹海峡航行风险， 海军护航 中国人民银行今日将美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8096（市场预期为6.7569） 日本5月出口超预期，增速创2022年底以来最快；新加坡5月出口激增38.4%，受AI需求推动创20年来最大增幅；日本4月核心机械订单激增，出口表现亦超预期；“OpenAI在2026年第一季度烧钱37亿美元” 预计欧洲央行9月还将加息一次，随后利率将在2027年前趋于平稳 路透社：受半导体需求提振，日本6月企业景气指数上升 中国出口洪流使欧盟关税壁垒在法国举行的七国集团（G7）会议上成为重要议题 新西兰第一季度经常账户赤字环比大幅收窄 美伊协议：当前进展及后续动向 日本央行加息后日元持续走弱，分析师关注干预风险 新西兰消费者信心指数跌至2023年以来最低水平，s 调查显示：investingLive美洲市场新闻综述：美联储公开市场委员会（FOMC）会议前夕，油价持续下跌 原油：私人库存调查显示，原油总库存降幅远超预期 澳大利亚储备银行（RBA）将利率维持在4.35%，暗示仍可能进一步加息——回顾 美国主要股指收于低点附近。道琼斯工业平均指数收高。摘要：尽管已签署谅解备忘录，美国仍在考虑派遣海军护航并强制要求购买保险，以解救滞留在霍尔木兹海峡的近500艘油轮；受供应恢复预期影响，油价走低；新西兰第二季度消费者信心指数跌至80.4，创两年新低； 第一季度经常账户赤字从之前的59.84亿新西兰元大幅收窄至10.08亿新西兰元。路透社日本短观调查：制造业指数从+8升至+13，非制造业指数从+29升至+32； 日经指数收复失地，创下接近70,000点的历史新高；新加坡NODX指数5月飙升38.4%，创20年来新高，主要受人工智能电子产品需求推动； 美国拟征收12.5%关税的提议给前景蒙上阴影；中国副总理宣布发行3000亿元人民币特别债券以补充银行资本，并承诺大力解决地方政府债务问题；中国人民银行行长潘功胜表示信贷增长不会恢复到以往的增速，并为人民币进一步国际化奠定了基础。 据路透社援引《The Information》报道，OpenAI在2026年第一季度烧掉了37亿美元，占其57亿美元收入的一半以上；上周提交的机密IPO招股说明书显示，该公司预计将于9月上市；在美联储公开市场委员会（FOMC）会议前夕，外汇市场表现平淡；中国股市表现落后，恒生指数下跌0.7%，上证综合指数 下跌0.2%，受汽车和铝业板块拖累，市场对美国推迟将DeepSeek及100多家中国企业列入黑名单的报道反应平淡。在等待北美交易时段开启之际，市场处于观望状态，美联储公开市场委员会（FOMC）的决议是主要事件风险，交易员不愿在美联储新任主席凯文·沃什首次面临重大政策考验之前扩大头寸。霍尔木兹海峡的供应形势仍是能源市场面临的关键宏观不确定因素。油价走低，因美伊临时和平协议继续被市场消化为供应将逐步恢复，尽管实际情况依然复杂：包括220艘油轮在内的近500艘船只正停泊在海峡外，无法通行，而保险市场对霍尔木兹海峡的过境运输仍处于实质性关闭状态。 特朗普政府目前正在考虑提供收费的海军护航服务，并可能动用《国防生产法》强制美国保险公司提供保险，因为白宫正面临将谅解备忘录转化为实际原油流的压力。亚太时段公布的数据总体积极。日本路透社“短观”调查显示，制造业信心连续两个月改善；受人工智能驱动的芯片需求推动，新加坡出口数据创下二十年来最高水平；新西兰经常账户赤字环比大幅收窄。唯一令人担忧的是新西兰消费者信心指数，受战争导致的燃料成本上涨和借贷压力影响，该指数跌至2023年以来的最低水平，家庭信心受到冲击。中国引发了显著的市场关注。副总理宣布发行3000亿元人民币特别债券以补充金融机构资本，并承诺大力化解地方政府债务，这是近期最具体的财政承诺。 中国人民银行行长潘功胜随后坦率地指出，维持中国此前信贷增长的速度既不可行也不可取，同时通过在上海推出新的离岸外汇和债券市场举措，为人民币更广泛的国际化奠定基础。在股市方面，日本日经225指数表现抢眼，收复了早盘跌幅，创下历史新高。中国股市表现相对滞后，恒生指数下跌0.7%，上证综合指数下跌0.2%。有报道称美国推迟将DeepSeek及100多家中国企业列入黑名单，但未能提振市场情绪。在宏观经济之外，OpenAI的财务状况成为市场焦点。路透社援引《The Information》的报道称，该公司2026年第一季度烧钱37亿美元，超过其57亿美元收入的一半。此前，该公司上周提交了保密的IPO招股说明书，预计将于9月上市。本文作者为investinglive.com的Eamonn Sheridan。

The disclosure follows last week's filing of a confidential IPO prospectus, with a market debut suspected for September. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
